title: Consultation: Guidelines for Canadian recreational water quality: Microbiological pathogens and biological hazards - Canada.ca
content:
## Consultation: Guidelines for Canadian recreational water quality: Microbiological pathogens and biological hazards

## Current status: Closed
This consultation ran from September 3, 2022, to November 2, 2022.
This guideline technical document evaluated the available information on microbiological pathogens and biological hazards in recreational waters. The goal of the document is to provide regulatory authorities and decision-makers with information on these topics. The purpose of this consultation is to seek comments on the proposed guidelines.

## Join in: how to participate
Send an email to water-eau@hc-sc.gc.ca with your ideas or comments to make yourself heard.

## Who is the focus of this consultation
We will engage with:
- federal, provincial and territorial regulatory and health protection agencies
- industry stakeholders
- academic communities
- non-governmental organizations
- members of the general public

## Key questions for discussion
We are looking for ideas and comments on the topic of:
- potential health risks from exposure to pathogenic microorganisms and other biological hazards associated with recreational water
We will use the input from this process to prepare and update the Guidelines for Canadian Recreational Water Quality .
Water and Air Quality Bureau Health Canada 269 Laurier Ave West Address Locator 4903B Ottawa ON  K1A 0K9 Email: water-eau@hc-sc.gc.ca
